description: Clothing in general: any cloth shaped and worn on the body — cloak, robe, shirt, wrap. Built visibly on 'wetha' (cloth): a garment is cloth that has entered relationship with a wearer. The general word for the clothing domain; specific garments can be composed from it ('sulae wethalu', a warm garment).
sound_symbolism: Begins as 'wetha' (cloth) begins — the soft weave of 'we' and the breathed 'tha' — then closes with 'lu', the held syllable: cloth taken up and kept close to the body. The derivation teaches itself: cloth, held, becomes clothing.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia wethalu phelu' (I wear a garment — wearing as holding, consistent with phelu's hold/have/keep range). 'shia wethalu to leiro' (they took off the garment). The fable's 'sulae wetha' (warm cloth) remains valid as poetic description; 'wethalu' is the everyday word.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Cloth spun, woven, and sewn by known hands — clothing as relationship with makers and materials, not disposable product. The word keeps the cloth visible inside the garment.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Clothing as the body's flowing ornament — drape and fold as living line.

tags.creation: Cloth shaped by craft into wearable form
